other_observers: Several, but none with birding expertise.; Norm Lewis
description: A large sparrow of Zonotrichia size. Clear breast, distinct lower wing bar and partial upper wing bar. Overall brownish-red color with heavily streaked upper back/shoulder area.   The most important area is the crown, with a yellow/gold patch at the forecrown, just above the bill and between the eyes, and the streaked rearcrown. These factors, coupled with the lack of streaking on the breast and flanks, suggest that this is a hatch-year bird which has mostly undergone its pre-basic molt.Behaviors: The bird was feeding on the ground with a variety of white-crowned sparrows. It was the same size as the white-crowns, but lacked the distinctive head pattern of either the juvenile or adult white-crowns, both of which were present.Call: SilentPlumage: Hatch year basic maybe
photographer: Photographer: Norm Lewis, Norm Lewis, Norm Lewis
